title: Decision Ledger Standard — Entry
description: An open schema for recording business decisions with their reasoning. Version 1.0 (June 2026). Published by Falcon Intelligence Group LLC under CC BY 4.0 — use it with or without ADAMAS.

id.description: Stable identifier: three-letter domain prefix + sequence number, e.g. SAL-021.

domain.description: The business domain the decision belongs to.

date.description: The date the decision was made (not when it was recorded).

title.description: One line stating the decision, phrased as the choice made.

context.description: The situation at the time of the call: constraints, pressures, what was known and not known. Written so a reader five years later understands why this was hard.

decision.description: The exact choice made, stated precisely enough to be falsifiable.

role.description: The role that made the call (role, not just a name — accountability must survive staff changes).

name.description: Optional: the person, for internal use.

dissent.description: Roles that disagreed at the time. Recording dissent is a feature: knowing who disagreed and why is gold later.

tradeoffs.description: What was knowingly given up, risked, or accepted. The most valuable field — it is what nobody writes down.

links.description: IDs of related decisions. Links are bi-directional: if A links B, B links A.

sources.description: Where the decision actually happened, as traceable references, e.g. 'email:2025-04-02#thread-114' or 'meeting:2025-04-11#weekly-review'.

status.description: Decisions are never deleted. A reversed decision stays in the ledger with status 'reversed' and a link to its successor.

superseded_by.description: If status is 'superseded' or 'reversed': the ID of the decision that replaced it.
